steps.title: Match the present-tense frame
steps.content: The passage describes a general present-tense situation: the programming languages have a difference and require technical knowledge. The plural subject laypeople needs a present-tense plural verb.

steps.title: Remove past-tense shifts
steps.content: B, C, and D all shift the sentence into past time, which does not fit the passage's general present-tense claim. The base present form agrees with the plural subject and keeps the tense consistent. The answer is A.
